title: silene ciliata subsp. graefferi
intro: Silene ciliata subsp. graefferi, also known as Graeffer's silene or Alpine catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Silene ciliata subsp. graefferi include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and soothe wounds, making it a popular remedy for skin conditions and digestive issues. Silene ciliata subsp. graefferi can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed in a variety of herbal preparations.
